author: wyf0912
text: .parameters() is empty in forward when using DataParallel

author: wyf0912
text:
## 🐛 Bug

## To Reproduce

code

```
import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.fc = nn.Linear(5, 1)
        self.cnn = nn.Conv2d(1, 1, 1, 1, 1)

    def forward(self, x):
        print('params list:', list(self.parameters()))
        print('params dict:', self._parameters.items())
        return x


if __name__ == '__main__':
    net = Net().cuda()
    net = nn.DataParallel(net)

    net(torch.rand(4, 5))
```

The output is as follows:

```
params list: []
params list: []
params dict: odict_items([])
params dict: odict_items([])
params list: []
params list: []
params dict: odict_items([])
params dict: odict_items([])
```

Both parameters() and \_parameters are empty.

## Expected behavior

Output the parameters of the module

author: ngimel
text: This is expected. `.parameters()` returns leaf variables, "parameters" in DataParallel replicas are not leafs (they are computed from the base model parameters.

author: wyf0912
text:
> This is expected. `.parameters()` returns leaf variables, "parameters" in DataParallel replicas are not leafs (they are computed from the base model parameters.

Thanks for your reply. I think it might be a good idea to introduce warnings or exceptions when using .parameters() for replicas.

author: kirill-ionkin
text:
Hello, guys! I think, a spent a lot of time, tried to get .parameters(), when parallel my network with DataParallel. And, i hope, this info will helpful for someone. In my opinion, you can get .parameters() correctry from paralleled model - and it depence on TORCH VESRION!

1. I tried to implement UGATIT to train it on multiple GPU's, but it doesn't work, because this issue - .parameters() was empty and nobody can help me!(torch=1.7)
2. After a lot of experiments, a change my enviroment with torch=1.2.0 and it works! I can get .parameters() and it is not empty!

# Above my steps inside jupyter-notebook

## You can try to run this on your notebook and see results

import torch  
import torch.nn as nn

torch.cuda.is\_available() # check cuda available  
!nvidia-smi

torch.**version** # check torch version

gpu\_ids = [0, 1] # set devices ids to use them in DataParallel

x = torch.rand(4, 10) # input into parallel model

### simple model implementation

```
class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, n_channels=10):
        super().__init__()
        self.lin1 = nn.Linear(n_channels, 10)
        self.lin2 = nn.Linear(10, 1)
        
    def forward(self, x):
        print(list(self.lin1.parameters())[0]) # to get patameters of fc layer
        return x
```

### create model

model = Model()  
model

### init fisrt device

device0 = torch.device("cuda:0")

model.to(device0)

### parallel model with DataParallel

model\_par = torch.nn.DataParallel(model, device\_ids=gpu\_ids)

model\_par(x)
